## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On April 22, 1970, the massive, highly historic, and deeply influential first Earth Day was officially and aggressively celebrated across the United States. Heavily championed by US Senator Gaylord Nelson, the massive environmental movement saw an estimated 20 million Americans aggressively take to the streets to protest massive industrial pollution and devastating ecological decay. The massive success of the event directly forced the aggressive creation of the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the massive passage of the Clean Air and Clean Water Acts.

On April 22, 1915, during the massive, horrific stalemate of World War I, the German Army aggressively and fundamentally revolutionized global warfare by executing the absolute, very first successful large-scale use of lethal poison gas. During the Second Battle of Ypres, German forces released a massive, terrifying cloud of chlorine gas against French colonial and Canadian troops. The horrific, massive deployment of chemical weapons heavily devastated the Allied lines, introducing a deeply terrifying, massive new dimension of mass destruction to modern combat.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On April 22, 1977, Morarji Desai, the massive, highly austere, and fiercely principled independence leader, aggressively commenced his tenure as the 4th Prime Minister of India. He achieved massive historical significance by heavily leading the Janata Party coalition to a massive, crushing electoral victory, officially ending the horrific, deeply oppressive period of Indira Gandhi's Emergency and massively restoring absolute democratic order and civil liberties to the Indian republic, though his massive coalition would soon fracture.

On April 22, 1914, B. R. Chopra, the massive, incredibly legendary, and highly influential Indian filmmaker and television director, was born in Ludhiana, Punjab. His massive, highly successful production house, B.R. Films, aggressively dominated Hindi cinema for decades. He is massively and universally revered for heavily directing incredibly socially conscious masterpieces like *Naya Daur* and for executing the massive, monumental, and historically defining television adaptation of the epic *Mahabharat*, which fundamentally unified the massive Indian television audience in the late 1980s.
